revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 矢塚男命 | 画像 = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | 画像サイズ = 270px | 画像説明 = <cente…」
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 矢塚男命 | 画像 = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | 画像サイズ = 270px | 画像説明 =
天白七五三社
（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内）
| 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 矢塚男命 | 別名 = 矢塚雄命 | 別称 = 蟹河原の長者 | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = 無名の娘 | 宮 = | 神社 = [[天白信仰|天白]]七五三社、達屋酢蔵神社 | 関連氏族 = 大矢嶋氏 }} '''矢塚男命'''（やつかおのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 ==概要== [[諏訪地方]]の先住神と伝わる。'''[[建御名方神]]'''（[[諏訪大社]]の祭神）に服従した'''[[洩矢神]]'''と戦った際に矢に当たって命を落としたと言われている宮坂光昭「古墳の変遷から見た古氏族の動向」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、79頁。野本三吉「天白論ノート―民衆信仰の源流―」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、251-252頁。宮地直一「[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1076393/39 諏訪地方の原始信仰]」『諏訪史 第2巻 前編』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年、62頁。。
永明村天白七五三社由緒ニ、字宮渡祭神矢塚男命此地ニ穴居ス、健御名方命此国ニ到リシ時洩矢神ト弓矢を以テ戦フ、矢塚男其矢ニ中リテ死セントシ、建御名方命ニ云フ、我ハ大神ニ随フベシ、一女アリ献ラムト言ヒ終テ死ストノ口伝アリ (下略){{Cite book|和書|author=山田肇|year=1929|title=諏訪大明神|publisher=信濃郷土文化普及会|pages=74-88|series=信濃郷土叢書 第1編|chapter=健御名方命に降服した諏訪の國つ神 洩矢神及び武居大伴主惠美志命}}延川和彦「[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/927368/21 信濃国官社諏訪神社神長官守矢家略系図]」『諏訪氏系図 続編』飯田好太郎、1921年、21コマ目。
[[File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|
達屋酢蔵神社
]] 民話では'''蟹河原'''（がにがわら）'''の長者'''とも称される。野馬を飼い馴らし、馬に乗って山へ狩りに出かけたり遠い国の市場で[[物々交換|物交]]してたりして、洩矢神さえも時には一歩を譲らなければいけないほどの権力者と描かれる。外来の建御名方神に敗れて降服した洩矢神を腰抜けと見なし、手下たちに罵倒させた。最初は馬耳東風と受け流したものの、宣戦布告の合図として建御名方神の居館に赤い矢が射ち込まれると、挑戦に応じた建御名方神と洩矢神は軍勢を率いて蟹河原長者の陣営を攻撃する。油断していた長者はすっかりと攻め立てられ、ついには流れ矢に当たってしまう。死ぬ間際に洩矢神に謝罪し、大切な娘を建御名方神に差し上げた。建御名方神はこの娘を家臣の[[彦狭知命]]{{efn|『[[古語拾遺]]』では、[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた時、[[讃岐忌部氏]]の祖先とされる手置帆負（たおきほおい）命とともに瑞殿（みずのみあらか）という御殿を造った神。ここでは建御名方神の殿を建てたとされる。達屋酢蔵神社の祭神の1柱でもある。}}に嫁がせ、2柱は蟹河原の領地に永住した今井野菊「がに河原長者」『諏訪ものがたり』甲陽書房、1960年、42-49頁。今井野菊「蟹河原長者」『神々の里 古代諏訪物語』国書刊行会、1976年、46-51頁。。 ==系譜== [[茅野市]]横内の大矢嶋氏の祖先神とされる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、147頁。。 ==神社== [[File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|
大天白社（茅野市ちの横内）
]] *'''天白七五三'''（しめ）'''社'''（'''天白〆社'''）（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内） :八塚雄命の旧地と伝わる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、143-144頁。。諏訪二十八天白の総社と言われている達屋酢蔵神社案内板。。 ::'''位置：'''{{coord|35|59|46.1|N|138|08|54.3|E|region:JP}} *'''[[達屋酢蔵神社]]'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） :諏訪大社上社の[[諏訪大社#摂末社|摂社]]・'''達屋社'''と'''酢蔵社'''の合併神社。 *'''大天白社'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） :横内の大矢嶋一族の祝神（[[氏神]]）。祭神は矢嶋祖神、[[天白神]]、[[池生神]]、[[白鳥神社|白鳥神]]、[[牛頭天王]]である。本殿は[[立川和四郎|立川和四郎富昌]]によるもので、1850年（[[嘉永]]3年）建造。2001年に茅野市有形文化財と指定された大天白社本殿案内板。。 ::'''位置：'''{{coord|35|59|43.9|N|138|08|53.6|E|region:JP}} == 注釈 == {{notelist}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} ==関連項目== *[[タケミナカタ]] *[[洩矢神]] *[[武居大伴主神]] *[[天白信仰]] {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかおのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつかおのみこと]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 矢塚男命 | 画像 = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | 画像サイズ = 270px | 画像説明 =
天白七五三社
（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内）

天白七五三社
（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内）
| 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 矢塚男命 | 別名 = 矢塚雄命 | 別称 = 蟹河原の長者 | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = 無名の娘 | 宮 = | 神社 = [[天白信仰|天白]]七五三社、達屋酢蔵神社 | 関連氏族 = 大矢嶋氏 }} '''矢塚男命'''（やつかおのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 ==概要== [[諏訪地方]]の先住神と伝わる。'''[[建御名方神]]'''（[[諏訪大社]]の祭神）に服従した'''[[洩矢神]]'''と戦った際に矢に当たって命を落としたと言われている宮坂光昭「古墳の変遷から見た古氏族の動向」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、79頁。野本三吉「天白論ノート―民衆信仰の源流―」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、251-252頁。宮地直一「[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1076393/39 諏訪地方の原始信仰]」『諏訪史 第2巻 前編』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年、62頁。。
永明村天白七五三社由緒ニ、字宮渡祭神矢塚男命此地ニ穴居ス、健御名方命此国ニ到リシ時洩矢神ト弓矢を以テ戦フ、矢塚男其矢ニ中リテ死セントシ、建御名方命ニ云フ、我ハ大神ニ随フベシ、一女アリ献ラムト言ヒ終テ死ストノ口伝アリ (下略){{Cite book|和書|author=山田肇|year=1929|title=諏訪大明神|publisher=信濃郷土文化普及会|pages=74-88|series=信濃郷土叢書 第1編|chapter=健御名方命に降服した諏訪の國つ神 洩矢神及び武居大伴主惠美志命}}延川和彦「[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/927368/21 信濃国官社諏訪神社神長官守矢家略系図]」『諏訪氏系図 続編』飯田好太郎、1921年、21コマ目。
[[File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|
達屋酢蔵神社
]] 民話では'''蟹河原'''（がにがわら）'''の長者'''とも称される。野馬を飼い馴らし、馬に乗って山へ狩りに出かけたり遠い国の市場で[[物々交換|物交]]してたりして、洩矢神さえも時には一歩を譲らなければいけないほどの権力者と描かれる。外来の建御名方神に敗れて降服した洩矢神を腰抜けと見なし、手下たちに罵倒させた。最初は馬耳東風と受け流したものの、宣戦布告の合図として建御名方神の居館に赤い矢が射ち込まれると、挑戦に応じた建御名方神と洩矢神は軍勢を率いて蟹河原長者の陣営を攻撃する。油断していた長者はすっかりと攻め立てられ、ついには流れ矢に当たってしまう。死ぬ間際に洩矢神に謝罪し、大切な娘を建御名方神に差し上げた。建御名方神はこの娘を家臣の[[彦狭知命]]{{efn|『[[古語拾遺]]』では、[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた時、[[讃岐忌部氏]]の祖先とされる手置帆負（たおきほおい）命とともに瑞殿（みずのみあらか）という御殿を造った神。ここでは建御名方神の殿を建てたとされる。達屋酢蔵神社の祭神の1柱でもある。}}に嫁がせ、2柱は蟹河原の領地に永住した今井野菊「がに河原長者」『諏訪ものがたり』甲陽書房、1960年、42-49頁。今井野菊「蟹河原長者」『神々の里 古代諏訪物語』国書刊行会、1976年、46-51頁。。 ==系譜== [[茅野市]]横内の大矢嶋氏の祖先神とされる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、147頁。。 [[File:%E4%B8%8A%E8%9F%B9%E6%B2%B3%E5%8E%9F%E9%81%BA%E8%B7%A1_-_Kami-Ganigawara_Ruins.jpg|thumb|200px|上蟹河原遺跡]] ==神社== [[File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|thumb|200px|
大天白社（茅野市ちの横内）
]] *'''天白七五三'''（しめ）'''社'''（'''天白〆社'''）（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内） :矢塚男命の旧地と伝わる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、143-144頁。。諏訪二十八天白の総社と言われている達屋酢蔵神社案内板。。 ::'''位置：''' {{Maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord= {{coord|35|59|46.1|N|138|08|54.3|E|region:JP}}marker=}} *'''[[達屋酢蔵神社]]'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） :諏訪大社上社の[[諏訪大社#摂末社|摂社]]・'''達屋社'''と'''酢蔵社'''の合併神社。 *'''大天白社'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） :横内の大矢嶋一族の祝神（[[氏神]]）。祭神は矢嶋祖神、[[天白神]]、[[池生神]]、[[白鳥神社|白鳥神]]、[[牛頭天王]]である。本殿は[[立川和四郎|立川和四郎富昌]]によるもので、1850年（[[嘉永]]3年）建造。2001年に茅野市有形文化財と指定された大天白社本殿案内板。。 ::'''位置：''' {{Maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord= {{coord|35|59|43.9|N|138|08|53.6|E|region:JP}}marker=}} == 注釈 == {{notelist}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} ==関連項目== *[[タケミナカタ]] *[[洩矢神]] *[[武居大伴主神]] *[[天白信仰]] {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかおのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつかおのみこと]]

永明村天白七五三社由緒ニ、字宮渡祭神矢塚男命此地ニ穴居ス、健御名方命此国ニ到リシ時洩矢神ト弓矢を以テ戦フ、矢塚男其矢ニ中リテ死セントシ、建御名方命ニ云フ、我ハ大神ニ随フベシ、一女アリ献ラムト言ヒ終テ死ストノ口伝アリ (下略){{Cite book|和書|author=山田肇|year=1929|title=諏訪大明神|publisher=信濃郷土文化普及会|pages=74-88|series=信濃郷土叢書 第1編|chapter=健御名方命に降服した諏訪の國つ神 洩矢神及び武居大伴主惠美志命}}延川和彦「[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/927368/21 信濃国官社諏訪神社神長官守矢家略系図]」『諏訪氏系図 続編』飯田好太郎、1921年、21コマ目。
[[File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|
達屋酢蔵神社

大天白社（茅野市ちの横内）
]] *'''天白七五三'''（しめ）'''社'''（'''天白〆社'''）（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内） :矢塚男命の旧地と伝わる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、143-144頁。。諏訪二十八天白の総社と言われている達屋酢蔵神社案内板。。 ::'''位置：''' {{Maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord= {{coord|35|59|46.1|N|138|08|54.3|E|region:JP}}marker=}} *'''[[達屋酢蔵神社]]'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） :諏訪大社上社の[[諏訪大社#摂末社|摂社]]・'''達屋社'''と'''酢蔵社'''の合併神社。 *'''大天白社'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） :横内の大矢嶋一族の祝神（[[氏神]]）。祭神は矢嶋祖神、[[天白神]]、[[池生神]]、[[白鳥神社|白鳥神]]、[[牛頭天王]]である。本殿は[[立川和四郎|立川和四郎富昌]]によるもので、1850年（[[嘉永]]3年）建造。2001年に茅野市有形文化財と指定された大天白社本殿案内板。。 ::'''位置：''' {{Maplink2|zoom=13|frame=yes|type=point|coord= {{coord|35|59|43.9|N|138|08|53.6|E|region:JP}}marker=}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} ==関連項目== *[[タケミナカタ]] *[[洩矢神]] *[[武居大伴主神]] *[[天白信仰]] {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかおのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつかおのみこと]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 矢塚男命 | 画像 = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | 画像サイズ = 270px | 画像説明 = 天白七五三社
（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内） | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 矢塚男命 | 別名 = 矢塚雄命 | 別称 = 蟹河原の長者 | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = 無名の娘 | 宮 = | 神社 = [[天白信仰|天白]]七五三社、達屋酢蔵神社 | 関連氏族 = 大矢嶋氏 }} '''矢塚男命'''（やつかおのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 ==概要== [[諏訪地方]]の先住神と伝わる。'''[[建御名方神]]'''（[[諏訪大社]]の祭神）に服従した'''[[洩矢神]]'''と戦った際に矢に当たって命を落としたと言われている宮坂光昭「古墳の変遷から見た古氏族の動向」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、79頁。野本三吉「天白論ノート―民衆信仰の源流―」『古諏訪の祭祀と氏族』 古部族研究会 編、人間社、2017年、251-252頁。宮地直一「[{{NDLDC|1076393/39}} 諏訪地方の原始信仰]」『諏訪史 第2巻 前編』信濃教育会諏訪部会、1931年、62頁。。
永明村天白七五三社由緒ニ、字宮渡祭神矢塚男命此地ニ穴居ス、健御名方命此国ニ到リシ時洩矢神ト弓矢を以テ戦フ、矢塚男其矢ニ中リテ死セントシ、建御名方命ニ云フ、我ハ大神ニ随フベシ、一女アリ献ラムト言ヒ終テ死ストノ口伝アリ (下略){{Cite book|和書|author=山田肇|year=1929|title=諏訪大明神|publisher=信濃郷土文化普及会|pages=74-88|series=信濃郷土叢書 第1編|chapter=健御名方命に降服した諏訪の國つ神 洩矢神及び武居大伴主惠美志命}}延川和彦「[{{NDLDC|927368/21}} 信濃国官社諏訪神社神長官守矢家略系図]」『諏訪氏系図 続編』飯田好太郎、1921年、21コマ目。
民話では'''蟹河原'''（がにがわら）'''の長者'''とも称される。野馬を飼い馴らし、馬に乗って山へ狩りに出かけたり遠い国の市場で[[物々交換|物交]]してたりして、洩矢神さえも時には一歩を譲らなければいけないほどの権力者と描かれる。外来の建御名方神に敗れて降服した洩矢神を腰抜けと見なし、手下たちに罵倒させた。最初は馬耳東風と受け流したものの、宣戦布告の合図として建御名方神の居館に赤い矢が射ち込まれると、挑戦に応じた建御名方神と洩矢神は軍勢を率いて蟹河原長者の陣営を攻撃する。油断していた長者はすっかりと攻め立てられ、ついには流れ矢に当たってしまう。死ぬ間際に洩矢神に謝罪し、大切な娘を建御名方神に差し上げた。建御名方神はこの娘を家臣の[[彦狭知命]]{{efn|『[[古語拾遺]]』では、[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた時、[[讃岐忌部氏]]の祖先とされる手置帆負（たおきほおい）命とともに瑞殿（みずのみあらか）という御殿を造った神。ここでは建御名方神の殿を建てたとされる。達屋酢蔵神社の祭神の1柱でもある。}}に嫁がせ、2柱は蟹河原の領地に永住した今井野菊「がに河原長者」『諏訪ものがたり』甲陽書房、1960年、42-49頁。今井野菊「蟹河原長者」『神々の里 古代諏訪物語』国書刊行会、1976年、46-51頁。。 ==系譜== [[茅野市]]横内の大矢嶋氏の祖先神とされる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、147頁。。 ==神社== *'''天白七五三'''（しめ）'''社'''（'''天白〆社'''）（[[長野県]][[茅野市]][[ちの町|ちの]]横内）{{ウィキ座標|35|59|46.1|N|138|08|54.3|E|region:JP|位置}} **矢塚男命の旧地と伝わる山田宗睦『天白紀行』人間社、2016年、143-144頁。。諏訪二十八天白の総社と言われている達屋酢蔵神社案内板。。 *'''[[達屋酢蔵神社]]'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内） **諏訪大社上社の[[諏訪大社#摂末社|摂社]]・'''達屋社'''と'''酢蔵社'''の合併神社。 *'''大天白社'''（長野県茅野市ちの横内）{{ウィキ座標|35|59|43.9|N|138|08|53.6|E|region:JP|位置}} **横内の大矢嶋一族の祝神（[[氏神]]）。祭神は矢嶋祖神、[[天白神]]、[[池生神]]、[[ヤマトタケル|白鳥神]]、[[牛頭天王]]である。本殿は[[立川和四郎|立川和四郎富昌]]によるもので、1850年（[[嘉永]]3年）建造。2001年に茅野市有形文化財と指定された大天白社本殿案内板。。 Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|達屋酢蔵神社 上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|上蟹河原遺跡 大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|大天白社（茅野市ちの横内） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} ==関連項目== *[[タケミナカタ]] *[[洩矢神]] *[[武居大伴主神]] *[[天白信仰]] {{諏訪信仰}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やつかおのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:諏訪信仰|神やつかおのみこと]]

==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:矢塚男命]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]]

{{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''[[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]]''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:dd]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:矢塚男命]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]]

{{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''[[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]]''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''[[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]]''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:矢塚男命]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]]

{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Trimming off this bad category]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] {{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also! == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}{{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}{{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:矢塚男命]] [[en:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]]

File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}{{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}{{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:矢塚男命]] [[en:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | image = 天白七五三社 - Tenpaku-Shime Shrine.jpg | image_size = 270px | caption = Tenpaku Shime Shrine
([[Chino, Nagano|Chino]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) | type = [[Kunitsukami]] | full_name = Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto | other_names = Yatsukao-no-Mikoto | alias = Elder of Ganigawara | rank = | function = | burial_place = | father = | mother = | consort = | children = Unnamed daughter | shrine = [[Tenpaku Shime Shrine]], [[Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine]] | related_clans = Oyajima Clan }} '''Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto''' (矢塚男命) is a deity of [[Kunitsukami]] in the folk traditions of [[Suwa faith|Suwa]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]]. == Overview == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is known as a predecessor deity in the [[Suwa region]] and is described as having perished during a battle with the god [[Moreya]] when [[Takeminakata]] (the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]]) took control of the region.
The tradition of Tenpaku Shime Shrine narrates: "The deity Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto once lived in this area. During the arrival of Takeminakata-no-Mikoto, a fierce battle ensued with Moreya, using bows and arrows. Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto was struck by an arrow and, in his final moments, pledged his allegiance to Takeminakata, offering his daughter before passing away."{{Cite book|author=Miyasaka Mitsuaki|title=Kofun no henshen kara mita koshi no doukou|year=2017|publisher=Ningensha|pages=79}}
Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is also known in folklore as the Elder of Ganigawara (蟹河原の長者). He is portrayed as a powerful figure who tamed wild horses, engaged in trade, and wielded considerable influence, even surpassing Moreya at times. However, his defiance of the newly arrived Takeminakata culminated in his defeat.{{Cite book|author=Miyachi Naoichi|title=Suwashi|year=1931|publisher=Shinano Kyōiku-kai|volume=2|page=62}} == Genealogy == Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestral deity of the Oyajima clan in [[Chino, Nagano]].{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|page=147}} == Shrines == * '''Tenpaku Shime Shrine''' (天白七五三社) ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - Recognized as the original dwelling of Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto. It is considered the main shrine of the 28 Tenpaku shrines in the Suwa region.{{Cite book|author=Yamada Shūmoku|title=Tenpaku Kiryō|year=2016|publisher=Ningensha|pages=143-144}} * '''{{ill|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine|ja|達屋酢蔵神社|12=draft}}''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - A merged shrine of Tatsuya and Sukura, both subordinate shrines of Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine. * '''Dai-Tenpaku Shrine''' ([[Chino, Nagano]]) - The tutelary shrine of the Oyajima family. The main deity is Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto, among other gods. The shrine's main hall, built in 1850 by the famed sculptor [[Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]], is designated as a cultural property of [[Chino City]].Dai-Tenpaku Shrine signboard. File:Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine , 達屋酢蔵神社 - panoramio.jpg|Tatsuya-Sukura Shrine File:上蟹河原遺跡 - Kami-Ganigawara Ruins.jpg|Kami-Ganigawara Ruins File:大天白社（茅野市）- Dai-Tenpaku Shrine.jpg|Dai-Tenpaku Shrine (Chino, Nagano) == See Also == * [[Takeminakata]] * [[Moreya]] * [[Suwa Taisha]] * [[Tenpaku faith]] {{citation needed|Yatsuka-o-no-Mikoto}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}{{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}}{{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}} {{citation needed|Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:矢塚男命]] [[en:Yazuka-Otoko-no-Mikoto]]
